content.text: What is Amazon Bedrock?

Amazon Bedrock is a fully managed service that offers a choice of high-performing foundation models from leading AI companies through a single API, along with capabilities to build generative AI applications with security, privacy, and responsible AI.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is a foundation model?

A foundation model is a large machine learning model pre-trained on a broad dataset that can be adapted (fine-tuned or prompted) for a wide range of downstream tasks such as text generation, summarization, classification, and image generation.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: Which foundation model providers are available through Amazon Bedrock?

Amazon Bedrock offers models from AI21 Labs (Jamba), Anthropic (Claude), Cohere (Command, Embed), Meta (Llama), Mistral AI (Mistral, Mixtral), Stability AI (Stable Diffusion), and Amazon (Titan Text, Titan Embeddings, Titan Image Generator).

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the Amazon Titan family of models?

Amazon Titan is a family of foundation models built by Amazon, available exclusively through Amazon Bedrock. It includes Titan Text for text generation, Titan Embeddings for creating vector representations, and Titan Image Generator for image creation.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What factors should you consider when selecting a foundation model for a use case?

Key factors include the task type (generation, classification, embedding), required languages, latency and throughput requirements, cost per token, context window size, model accuracy for your domain, and any compliance or data residency constraints.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the difference between a text generation model and an embedding model?

A text generation model produces human-readable text output given a prompt, while an embedding model converts text into fixed-length numerical vectors that capture semantic meaning, used for similarity search, clustering, and retrieval tasks.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is model fine-tuning in Amazon Bedrock?

Model fine-tuning in Amazon Bedrock lets you customize a foundation model using your own labeled training data, producing a custom model that is more accurate for your specific domain or task while retaining the base model's general capabilities.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What training data format does Amazon Bedrock expect for fine-tuning?

Amazon Bedrock expects training data in JSONL format, where each line is a JSON object with a prompt and completion field. The data must be stored in an Amazon S3 bucket accessible to the Bedrock service role.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is continued pre-training in Amazon Bedrock?

Continued pre-training allows you to provide additional unlabeled, domain-specific data to a foundation model so it learns new domain knowledge. Unlike fine-tuning, it does not require prompt-completion pairs — it uses raw text to extend the model's pre-training.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the difference between fine-tuning and continued pre-training?

Fine-tuning uses labeled prompt-completion pairs to improve a model's performance on a specific task, while continued pre-training uses unlabeled text to expand the model's domain knowledge without targeting a particular task format.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the InvokeModel API in Amazon Bedrock?

InvokeModel is a Bedrock runtime API that sends a prompt to a specified foundation model and returns the generated response. The request and response body formats are model-specific, requiring you to know each provider's input schema.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the Converse API in Amazon Bedrock?

The Converse API is a unified, model-agnostic API in Amazon Bedrock that provides a consistent request and response format across all supported models, simplifying multi-turn conversations and making it easier to switch between models without code changes.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the advantage of the Converse API over InvokeModel?

The Converse API provides a consistent interface across all Bedrock models, so you do not need to learn or handle provider-specific request and response formats. It also supports multi-turn conversations, tool use, and system prompts in a standardized way.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What are inference parameters in Amazon Bedrock?

Inference parameters control model output behavior. Common parameters include temperature (randomness), top_p (nucleus sampling), max_tokens (output length limit), and stop_sequences (tokens that halt generation).

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What does the temperature parameter control in a foundation model?

Temperature controls the randomness of the model's output. A lower temperature (e.g., 0) produces more deterministic and focused responses, while a higher temperature (e.g., 1) produces more diverse and creative responses.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is top_p (nucleus sampling)?

Top_p sampling limits the model's token selection to the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability exceeds the threshold p. For example, top_p=0.9 means the model considers only the top tokens that together account for 90% of the probability mass.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is a context window in a foundation model?

A context window is the maximum number of tokens (input plus output) that a model can process in a single request. Larger context windows allow the model to handle longer documents or conversations but may increase latency and cost.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is model evaluation in Amazon Bedrock?

Model evaluation in Amazon Bedrock lets you assess and compare foundation model outputs using automatic metrics (such as ROUGE, BERTScore) or human evaluation workflows, helping you select the best model for your use case.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What are Amazon Bedrock Guardrails?

Amazon Bedrock Guardrails is a feature that lets you implement application-specific safeguards by defining denied topics, content filters, word filters, sensitive information filters (PII), and contextual grounding checks to control model inputs and outputs.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is Amazon Titan Embeddings?

Amazon Titan Embeddings is a foundation model in Bedrock that converts text into numerical vector representations (embeddings) of 1024 or 256 dimensions, used for semantic search, retrieval-augmented generation, and similarity comparisons.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the difference between Amazon Titan Text Embeddings V1 and V2?

Titan Text Embeddings V2 adds support for configurable output dimensions (256, 512, or 1024), unit-length normalization, and improved multilingual performance compared to V1, which outputs fixed 1536-dimensional vectors.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is a custom model in Amazon Bedrock?

A custom model is a privately fine-tuned or continued pre-trained version of a Bedrock base model, stored in your account. It is accessible only to you and can be deployed via provisioned throughput for inference.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How do you deploy a custom model for inference in Amazon Bedrock?

After fine-tuning completes, you must purchase provisioned throughput for the custom model to make it available for inference. On-demand pricing is not available for custom models — only provisioned throughput with a commitment term.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the role of a hyperparameter in model fine-tuning?

Hyperparameters control the fine-tuning process. Key hyperparameters include learning rate (step size for weight updates), epoch count (number of passes through the training data), and batch size (number of samples per training step).

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is model distillation in Amazon Bedrock?

Model distillation in Bedrock allows you to transfer knowledge from a larger, more capable teacher model to a smaller, faster student model. This produces a smaller model that approximates the teacher's performance at lower cost and latency.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What are the supported base models for fine-tuning in Amazon Bedrock?

Supported base models for fine-tuning vary by region and update over time, but commonly include Amazon Titan Text, Cohere Command, and Meta Llama models. You should check the Bedrock console or documentation for the current list of fine-tunable models.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)?

RAG is a pattern that combines a retrieval system (such as a vector database) with a generative model. Relevant documents are retrieved based on the user's query and included in the model's context, grounding the response in factual source material.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases?

Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases is a fully managed RAG feature that automatically ingests, chunks, and embeds your documents, stores them in a vector database, and retrieves relevant passages at query time to augment foundation model responses.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What data sources can Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases ingest?

Knowledge Bases can ingest documents from Amazon S3, and also supports connectors for Confluence, SharePoint, Salesforce, and web crawlers. Supported file formats include PDF, TXT, MD, HTML, DOC/DOCX, CSV, and XLS/XLSX.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What vector stores does Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases support?

Knowledge Bases supports Amazon OpenSearch Serverless, Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL (with pgvector), Pinecone, Redis Enterprise Cloud, and MongoDB Atlas as vector store backends for storing and searching document embeddings.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is chunking in the context of RAG?

Chunking is the process of splitting large documents into smaller segments (chunks) before embedding. Proper chunking ensures that each vector represents a coherent unit of information, improving retrieval accuracy in RAG pipelines.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What chunking strategies does Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases support?

Knowledge Bases supports fixed-size chunking (by token count), default chunking (approximately 300 tokens with overlap), no chunking (each file is one chunk), semantic chunking (based on meaning boundaries), and hierarchical chunking.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is Amazon Bedrock Agents?

Amazon Bedrock Agents is a fully managed capability that lets foundation models dynamically plan and execute multi-step tasks by invoking APIs, querying knowledge bases, and calling AWS Lambda functions, orchestrated by a ReAct-style reasoning loop.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How does an Amazon Bedrock Agent decide which action to take?

Bedrock Agents use a ReAct (Reasoning and Acting) approach where the model reasons about the user's request, selects an appropriate action group or knowledge base to call, observes the result, and repeats until it can provide a final answer.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is an action group in Amazon Bedrock Agents?

An action group defines a set of actions that an agent can perform. It consists of an OpenAPI schema describing the available API operations and an AWS Lambda function that implements those operations.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How do you provide an API schema for an Amazon Bedrock Agent action group?
You provide an OpenAPI 3.0 schema (in JSON or YAML) that describes the API endpoints, parameters, and response formats. The agent uses this schema to understand when and how to call each action.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is prompt engineering?
Prompt engineering is the practice of designing and refining the text input (prompt) given to a foundation model to elicit the most accurate, relevant, and useful output for a specific task.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is a system prompt?

A system prompt is an instruction provided at the beginning of a conversation that sets the model's behavior, persona, tone, or constraints for all subsequent turns. It is separate from user messages and is supported by the Converse API.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is few-shot prompting?

Few-shot prompting is a technique where you include several input-output examples in the prompt before the actual query. These examples help the model understand the expected format, style, and task without any fine-tuning.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is chain-of-thought prompting?

Chain-of-thought prompting instructs the model to show its reasoning step by step before arriving at a final answer. This technique improves accuracy on complex reasoning tasks like math, logic, and multi-step analysis.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is zero-shot prompting?

Zero-shot prompting provides a task instruction to the model without any examples. The model relies entirely on its pre-trained knowledge to generate a response. It works best for straightforward, well-understood tasks.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How can AWS Lambda be used with Amazon Bedrock?

AWS Lambda can invoke Bedrock APIs (InvokeModel, Converse) to add generative AI capabilities to serverless applications. Lambda functions also serve as the execution backend for Bedrock Agent action groups.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How can AWS Step Functions orchestrate generative AI workflows?

AWS Step Functions can coordinate multi-step generative AI pipelines by chaining Bedrock API calls, Lambda functions, S3 operations, and other AWS services into a visual workflow with error handling, retries, and parallel execution.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the Bedrock integration in AWS Step Functions?

Step Functions provides an optimized integration with Amazon Bedrock that allows you to call InvokeModel directly from a state machine definition without writing Lambda code, using the Task state with the bedrock:invokeModel resource.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is Amazon S3's role in a RAG architecture?

Amazon S3 serves as the primary document storage layer in a RAG architecture. Source documents (PDFs, text files, HTML) are stored in S3 and ingested by Knowledge Bases or custom pipelines for chunking, embedding, and indexing.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is Amazon OpenSearch Serverless and how is it used with Bedrock?

Amazon OpenSearch Serverless is a serverless deployment option for OpenSearch that provides vector search capabilities. It is one of the supported vector stores for Bedrock Knowledge Bases, storing and retrieving document embeddings for RAG.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is a vector index in OpenSearch Serverless?

A vector index is an OpenSearch index configured with a k-NN (k-nearest neighbors) field that stores embeddings and supports approximate nearest neighbor search, enabling fast semantic retrieval of similar documents.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How do you connect a Knowledge Base to a Bedrock Agent?

You associate a Knowledge Base with an agent during agent creation or update. The agent can then query the Knowledge Base as one of its available tools, retrieving relevant passages to ground its responses.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the RetrieveAndGenerate API in Amazon Bedrock?

RetrieveAndGenerate is a Knowledge Bases API that performs end-to-end RAG in a single call — it retrieves relevant chunks from the vector store, constructs a prompt with the retrieved context, and invokes a foundation model to generate a grounded response.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the Retrieve API in Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases?

The Retrieve API returns the relevant document chunks from the Knowledge Base vector store without generating a model response. This gives you control over how retrieved passages are used in your own prompt template and model invocation.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is a guardrail policy in Amazon Bedrock?

A guardrail policy is a configuration that defines content filters, denied topics, word filters, sensitive information filters, and contextual grounding checks. You create a guardrail with a specific version and apply it to model invocations.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How do you apply a guardrail to model invocations in Bedrock?

You specify the guardrail ID and version in the InvokeModel or Converse API request using the guardrailIdentifier and guardrailVersion parameters. The guardrail evaluates both the input prompt and the model output.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is prompt injection and how does Bedrock help prevent it?

Prompt injection is an attack where malicious instructions are embedded in user input to manipulate model behavior. Bedrock Guardrails can detect and block prompt injection attempts using input content filters and denied topic policies.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is Amazon CloudWatch's role in monitoring Bedrock?

Amazon CloudWatch collects Bedrock runtime metrics such as invocation count, latency, throttled requests, and input/output token counts. You can create dashboards and alarms to monitor model usage and performance.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is provisioned throughput in Amazon Bedrock?

Provisioned throughput reserves dedicated inference capacity for a specific model, guaranteeing consistent performance with no throttling. It is required for custom (fine-tuned) models and is available in 1-month or 6-month commitment terms.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is on-demand pricing in Amazon Bedrock?

On-demand pricing charges per input and output token processed, with no upfront commitment. It is the default pricing model for base foundation models in Bedrock and is suitable for variable or unpredictable workloads.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is batch inference in Amazon Bedrock?

Batch inference lets you submit a large set of prompts as a single batch job in Bedrock. The service processes them asynchronously and stores results in S3, offering up to 50% cost savings compared to on-demand invocations for large-scale workloads.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How do you submit a batch inference job in Amazon Bedrock?

You prepare a JSONL file with one request per line, upload it to S3, and call the CreateModelInvocationJob API specifying the model ID, input S3 URI, and output S3 URI. Bedrock processes the requests and writes results to the output location.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is model invocation logging in Amazon Bedrock?

Model invocation logging captures the full request and response payloads for Bedrock API calls. Logs can be delivered to Amazon S3 and/or Amazon CloudWatch Logs for auditing, debugging, and compliance purposes.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is prompt caching in Amazon Bedrock?

Prompt caching stores and reuses the computed key-value representations of frequently used prompt prefixes (such as system prompts or long context), reducing latency by up to 85% and cost by up to 90% for repeated prompt patterns.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What evaluation metrics are commonly used for generative AI models?

Common metrics include ROUGE (for summarization overlap), BERTScore (semantic similarity), perplexity (model confidence), human evaluation ratings (helpfulness, harmlessness, honesty), and task-specific accuracy or F1 scores.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is ROUGE and when is it used?

ROUGE (Recall-Oriented Understudy for Gisting Evaluation) is a set of metrics that measure the overlap between model-generated text and reference text. It is commonly used to evaluate summarization quality by comparing n-gram matches.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is BERTScore?

BERTScore is an evaluation metric that uses contextual embeddings from a BERT model to compute the semantic similarity between generated text and reference text. It captures meaning beyond exact word overlap, making it more robust than ROUGE for paraphrases.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is model latency and how can it be reduced in Bedrock?

Model latency is the time from sending a request to receiving the complete response. It can be reduced by using provisioned throughput, enabling response streaming, choosing smaller or faster models, reducing output token limits, and using prompt caching.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is response streaming in Amazon Bedrock?

Response streaming returns model output tokens incrementally as they are generated, rather than waiting for the full response. This reduces perceived latency for the end user. It is available via the InvokeModelWithResponseStream and ConverseStream APIs.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How do you optimize costs for Bedrock inference?

Cost optimization strategies include choosing the right-sized model for your task, using batch inference for bulk workloads, enabling prompt caching for repeated patterns, reducing unnecessary output tokens, and purchasing provisioned throughput for sustained workloads.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is Amazon Bedrock model access and how do you configure it?

Model access is a Bedrock console setting where you request access to specific foundation models. Models are not available by default — you must explicitly enable each model you want to use in each AWS region through the model access page.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the AWS Shared Responsibility Model for generative AI?

AWS is responsible for the security of the cloud infrastructure and managed AI services, while the customer is responsible for their data, model inputs/outputs, access controls, prompt content, application logic, and compliance with applicable regulations.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What IAM permissions are needed to invoke Amazon Bedrock models?

You need the bedrock:InvokeModel permission on the model resource ARN. For streaming, you also need bedrock:InvokeModelWithResponseStream. For Knowledge Bases, additional permissions like bedrock:Retrieve and bedrock:RetrieveAndGenerate are required.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is a Bedrock service role?

A Bedrock service role is an IAM role that Amazon Bedrock assumes to access your resources, such as reading training data from S3 for fine-tuning, writing model artifacts, or accessing vector stores for Knowledge Bases.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How do you use VPC endpoints with Amazon Bedrock?

You create an interface VPC endpoint for the Bedrock runtime service (com.amazonaws.region.bedrock-runtime) in your VPC. This allows your VPC resources to call Bedrock APIs over AWS PrivateLink without traversing the public internet.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is AWS PrivateLink and why is it important for Bedrock?

AWS PrivateLink provides private connectivity between VPCs and AWS services. For Bedrock, it ensures that model invocation traffic stays within the AWS network, meeting compliance requirements for sensitive workloads that must not traverse the internet.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How does Amazon Bedrock handle data privacy?

Amazon Bedrock does not use your inputs or outputs to train or improve base foundation models. Your data is encrypted in transit and at rest, remains within your chosen AWS region, and you retain full ownership and control over your data.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is AWS CloudTrail's role with Amazon Bedrock?

AWS CloudTrail logs all Bedrock management API calls (creating models, configuring guardrails) and can log runtime data events (model invocations) when enabled. This provides an audit trail for security investigations and compliance.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How do you encrypt data at rest in Amazon Bedrock?

Amazon Bedrock encrypts data at rest by default using AWS-managed keys. For additional control, you can specify a customer-managed AWS KMS key to encrypt custom models, fine-tuning jobs, Knowledge Base data stores, and model invocation logs.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is AWS KMS and how is it used with Bedrock?

AWS Key Management Service (KMS) lets you create and manage encryption keys. With Bedrock, you can use a customer-managed KMS key to encrypt custom model artifacts, training data, Knowledge Base indices, and invocation log data for greater control.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How do you detect and redact PII in Amazon Bedrock?

Bedrock Guardrails includes a sensitive information filter that can detect and redact personally identifiable information (PII) such as names, email addresses, phone numbers, and Social Security numbers in both model inputs and outputs.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is a denied topic in Amazon Bedrock Guardrails?

A denied topic is a subject that you define in a guardrail to prevent the model from generating content about it. When a denied topic is detected in the input or output, Bedrock blocks the response and returns a predefined message.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is a content filter in Amazon Bedrock Guardrails?

A content filter evaluates model inputs and outputs for harmful categories such as hate speech, insults, sexual content, violence, and misconduct. You configure the filter strength (none, low, medium, high) for each category.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is contextual grounding in Amazon Bedrock Guardrails?

Contextual grounding checks verify that the model's response is grounded in the provided source material (e.g., retrieved RAG documents) and relevant to the user's query. It helps detect and block hallucinated responses.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How do you control access to specific Bedrock models using IAM?

You use IAM policies with resource-level permissions specifying the model ARN. For example, you can allow bedrock:InvokeModel only on specific model IDs, preventing users from accessing models that are not approved for their use case.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is an IAM policy condition key for Amazon Bedrock?

Bedrock supports IAM condition keys such as bedrock:GuardrailIdentifier (requiring a specific guardrail) and aws:RequestedRegion (restricting by region), allowing fine-grained access control over model invocations.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the purpose of tagging resources in Amazon Bedrock?

Tags are key-value pairs you assign to Bedrock resources (custom models, provisioned throughput, agents) to organize resources, control access via tag-based IAM policies, and allocate costs using AWS Cost Explorer.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is Amazon Bedrock Marketplace?

Amazon Bedrock Marketplace allows you to discover and subscribe to additional foundation models from third-party providers directly through the Bedrock console, extending the range of models available beyond the default Bedrock model catalog.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the role of embeddings in a RAG pipeline?

Embeddings convert text (both documents and queries) into numerical vectors that capture semantic meaning. At query time, the query embedding is compared against stored document embeddings using similarity search to find the most relevant passages.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What similarity metrics are used for vector search?

Common similarity metrics include cosine similarity (measures the angle between vectors), Euclidean distance (L2 norm, measures geometric distance), and dot product (measures alignment). Cosine similarity is most commonly used for text embeddings.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the maximum number of tokens for Amazon Titan Text Embeddings V2 input?
Amazon Titan Text Embeddings V2 supports a maximum input length of 8,192 tokens. Texts exceeding this limit must be truncated or split into smaller chunks before embedding.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is PartyRock?

PartyRock is an Amazon Bedrock playground that allows you to experiment with foundation models and build generative AI applications through a no-code, shareable web interface without needing an AWS account.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is Amazon Q Developer?

Amazon Q Developer is a generative AI-powered assistant for software development that provides code suggestions, answers questions about AWS services, helps debug and transform code, and integrates with IDEs and the AWS Management Console.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the difference between Amazon Bedrock and Amazon SageMaker for generative AI?

Amazon Bedrock is a fully managed service for accessing and customizing foundation models via API with no infrastructure management. Amazon SageMaker provides more control for training, hosting, and managing custom ML models, including the full ML lifecycle.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is Amazon SageMaker JumpStart?

SageMaker JumpStart is a machine learning hub within SageMaker that provides pre-trained foundation models, built-in algorithms, and solution templates. It allows you to deploy models to SageMaker endpoints with more infrastructure control than Bedrock.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is responsible AI and how does AWS address it?

Responsible AI involves developing and deploying AI systems that are fair, transparent, safe, and accountable. AWS addresses it through Bedrock Guardrails, model evaluation, watermark detection for Titan Image Generator, and documentation of model capabilities and limitations.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is hallucination in generative AI?

Hallucination occurs when a model generates content that is factually incorrect, fabricated, or unsupported by the input context. RAG, contextual grounding checks, and temperature tuning are common techniques to reduce hallucinations.

content.text: How does RAG help reduce hallucination?

RAG provides the model with relevant source documents as context alongside the user's query. By grounding the response in factual reference material, the model is less likely to generate fabricated or incorrect information.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is an embedding dimension and why does it matter?

Embedding dimension is the length of the vector produced by an embedding model. Higher dimensions capture more nuance but require more storage and compute. Titan Embeddings V2 lets you choose 256, 512, or 1024 dimensions to balance accuracy and efficiency.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the purpose of overlap in chunking for RAG?

Chunk overlap ensures that information spanning a chunk boundary is captured in at least one complete chunk. This prevents losing context at boundaries and improves retrieval quality by making each chunk more self-contained.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What AWS services can trigger a Bedrock Agent?

A Bedrock Agent can be invoked from any service that can make API calls, including AWS Lambda, Amazon Lex (for conversational interfaces), Amazon API Gateway (for REST APIs), and directly from applications using the Bedrock Agent Runtime API.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the InvokeAgent API?

InvokeAgent is the Bedrock Agent Runtime API used to send a user utterance to a deployed agent. The agent processes the request through its reasoning loop, invokes action groups or knowledge bases as needed, and returns the final response.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What format does the model invocation logging output use?

Model invocation logs are JSON records that include the request timestamp, model ID, input prompt, output completion, token counts, latency, and status code. They are delivered to S3 as JSON files or to CloudWatch Logs as log events.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the purpose of model evaluation jobs in Amazon Bedrock?

Model evaluation jobs let you systematically compare the performance of multiple foundation models on your specific tasks using automatic metrics or human reviewers, helping you make data-driven decisions about which model to deploy.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is a model evaluation report in Amazon Bedrock?

A model evaluation report summarizes the results of an evaluation job, including metric scores (e.g., ROUGE, accuracy, toxicity) across all evaluated models and task types, stored in S3 as a JSON file that can be viewed in the Bedrock console.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How can you compare models using Amazon Bedrock?

You can create a model evaluation job that runs the same set of prompts against multiple models and compares their outputs using automatic metrics (ROUGE, BERTScore, accuracy) or human evaluation ratings for quality, helpfulness, and safety.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is token-based pricing in Amazon Bedrock?

Token-based pricing charges separately for input tokens (prompt) and output tokens (completion). Rates vary by model and are typically listed per 1,000 tokens. Input tokens are generally cheaper than output tokens.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the purpose of max_tokens in a Bedrock API call?

The max_tokens parameter sets the maximum number of tokens the model will generate in its response. Setting an appropriate limit controls cost and prevents unnecessarily long outputs while ensuring the response is complete enough for the task.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is Amazon Bedrock Flows?

Amazon Bedrock Flows is a visual builder that lets you create and orchestrate generative AI workflows by connecting foundation models, prompts, knowledge bases, agents, and AWS services in a directed acyclic graph (DAG) without writing code.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What are stop sequences in Amazon Bedrock?

Stop sequences are specified strings that, when generated by the model, signal it to stop producing further output. They are useful for controlling output format, such as stopping after generating a specific delimiter or closing tag.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the difference between synchronous and asynchronous inference in Bedrock?

Synchronous inference (InvokeModel, Converse) returns the response in real time and is suited for interactive applications. Asynchronous inference (batch jobs) processes requests in the background and stores results in S3, suited for bulk processing.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: How does Amazon Bedrock ensure model isolation between customers?

Amazon Bedrock processes each customer's requests in isolated compute environments. Customer data is not shared across accounts, is encrypted in transit and at rest, and is not used to train or improve the base foundation models.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the Bedrock runtime endpoint?

The Bedrock runtime endpoint (bedrock-runtime.region.amazonaws.com) is the API endpoint used for model inference operations like InvokeModel, Converse, and ConverseStream. It is separate from the Bedrock control plane endpoint used for management operations.

system.text: You are an AWS expert. Answer questions about AWS services accurately and concisely.

content.text: What is the Bedrock control plane endpoint used for?

The Bedrock control plane endpoint (bedrock.region.amazonaws.com) handles management operations such as listing models, creating fine-tuning jobs, managing guardrails, configuring knowledge bases, and setting up agents.
